event.title: UN Adds Israel and Russia to Sexual Violence in Conflict Blacklist
event.summary: The United Nations published its annual Conflict-Related Sexual Violence report, adding Israel and Russia to a blacklist of parties committing sexual violence in warzones for the first time. The report verified 31 cases of sexual violence by Israeli forces against Palestinians and 310 cases by Russian forces against Ukrainians, prompting diplomatic backlash from both countries.

sources.author: Emma Graham-Harrison Chief Middle East correspondent
sources.author_background: Emma Graham-Harrison is The Guardian's Chief Middle East correspondent who has reported extensively from conflict zones including Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan, and won multiple awards for her coverage of human rights issues in the region.

sources.ownership: Majority owned by the Schocken family. M. DuMont Schauberg, a German publisher, holds a minority stake.
Owned by Thomson Reuters Corporation, a publicly traded Canadian-American media conglomerate.
Majority owned by the Schocken family. M. DuMont Schauberg, a German publisher, holds a minority stake.

claims.text: The UN added Israel to its sexual violence in conflict blacklist for the first time.
claims.rationale: All six sources across center, center-left, and center-right outlets confirm this UN action, providing strong cross-spectrum agreement.

framing_variants.characterization: Straightforward factual statement: Israel added to UN blacklist for the first time
UN added Israel and Russia to a blacklist for sexual violence in conflict
UN report accuses Israeli forces of rape and sexual abuse of Palestinian detainees
The UN placed Israel on a sexual violence blacklist
UN adds Israel to sexual violence in conflict zones blacklist
Israel was blacklisted by a UN body for sex crimes

claims.text: The UN also added Russia to its sexual violence in conflict blacklist for the first time.
claims.rationale: Center (BBC, Reuters) and center-left (Guardian) outlets all confirm Russia's addition to the blacklist, providing cross-spectrum agreement.

framing_variants.characterization: Russian armed and security forces were blacklisted for the first time for sexual violence in Ukraine
The UN added Russia to a blacklist for sexual violence in conflict
The UN placed Russia on a sexual violence blacklist

claims.text: The UN report lists 77 countries and armed groups in total.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian specifies the total number of parties on the blacklist.

framing_variants.characterization: The report lists 77 countries and armed groups

claims.text: A UN official stated they did not review evidence before blacklisting Israel.
claims.rationale: Only i24 News reports this claim about a UN official's admission regarding procedural conduct; no other source confirms or contradicts it.

framing_variants.characterization: i24 News frames this as a notable admission of procedural failures in the blacklisting decision

claims.text: Details of the UN report were shared by Israeli diplomats at the UN before its official release, and the full report was subsequently posted online by the US news site PassBlue.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports the pre-release disclosure by Israeli diplomats and the subsequent online posting by PassBlue.

framing_variants.characterization: Details were shared by Israeli diplomats before release; full report posted by PassBlue

claims.text: The UN verified 31 cases of conflict-related sexual violence perpetrated by Israeli forces against Palestinians from Gaza and the West Bank.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) confirm the verified figure of 31 cases, providing cross-spectrum agreement on the core finding.

framing_variants.characterization: UN had verified 31 cases of sexual violence perpetrated by Israeli forces against Palestinians
The UN verified sexual abuse of 31 Palestinian men, women and children from the Gaza Strip and the occupied West Bank between 2023 and 2025

claims.text: Of the 31 verified cases, 13 occurred in 2025 and 18 occurred in the two prior years.
claims.rationale: Only BBC provides this temporal breakdown of the 31 verified cases.

framing_variants.characterization: 13 cases in 2025; 18 in the two prior years

claims.text: The verified cases of Israeli sexual violence affected 14 men, 7 women, 9 boys, and 1 girl.
claims.rationale: Only BBC provides this full demographic breakdown of verified Israeli sexual violence victims.

framing_variants.characterization: Verified violations carried out against 14 men, seven women, nine boys and one girl

claims.text: Verified violations by Israeli forces included rape, gang rape, rape with objects, forced nudity, attacks on and targeted shooting of genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, threats of rape, and attempted rape.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) document overlapping lists of verified violation types, providing cross-spectrum corroboration.

framing_variants.characterization: Violations included rape, gang rape, forced nudity, and violence to genitals
Includes repeated gang-rapes, use as torture, rape with objects, attempted rape, attacks on genitals, targeted shooting of genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, forced nudity, threats of rape

claims.text: Perpetrators of Israeli sexual violence included Israeli military officers, police, prison service officers, and members of an elite police counter-terrorism unit.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) identify overlapping categories of perpetrators across Israeli security institutions.

framing_variants.characterization: Committed by officers from the Israeli military, police and prison service
Perpetrators included Israeli soldiers, prison officers, and members of an elite police counter-terrorism unit

claims.text: The UN documented nine cases of rape by Israeli forces, mostly involving men or boys from Gaza, targeted in detention centres or during interrogation.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian provides this specific breakdown of rape cases and their contexts.

framing_variants.characterization: Nine cases of rape by Israeli forces, mostly of men or boys from Gaza, targeted in detention centres or during interrogation

claims.text: One rape attack by Israeli forces took place in a police station in the Gush Etzion settlement in the occupied West Bank.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian provides this specific location detail for one of the documented attacks.

framing_variants.characterization: One attack took place in a police station in the Gush Etzion settlement in the occupied West Bank

claims.text: The UN described the documented cases as indicative of incidents and patterns rather than a comprehensive summary of conflict-related sexual violence by Israeli forces.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian includes this UN framing caveat about the scope of the documented cases.

framing_variants.characterization: Cases described as 'indicative of incidents and patterns' rather than a comprehensive summary

claims.text: The UN said the Israeli government obstructed its attempts to fully investigate sexual violence, including barring UN experts from detention centres and blocking travel to Gaza.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the UN's finding of Israeli obstruction, providing cross-spectrum confirmation.

framing_variants.characterization: UN said its attempts to fully investigate the issue had been obstructed by the Israeli government
Israel barred UN experts from detention centres and blocked travel to Gaza

claims.text: The UN said threats were made against Palestinian detainees to prevent them from reporting abuse after their release.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the UN's finding of threats against detainees.

framing_variants.characterization: Threats made against detainees to prevent abuse being reported
Israel has threatened Palestinian detainees if they report abuse after their release

claims.text: Israel has not allowed the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit detainees since October 2023.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports this specific restriction on ICRC access to detainees.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel has not allowed the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit detainees since October 2023

claims.text: The UN was unable to verify allegations of sexual violence by Hamas in part because the Israeli government denied access to UN bodies to carry out investigations.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports this specific connection between Israeli access restrictions and the inability to verify Hamas allegations.

framing_variants.characterization: UN unable to verify Hamas reports as the Israeli government denied access to UN bodies to carry out investigations

claims.text: Israel rejected the allegations of sexual abuse included in the UN report.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report Israel's denial, providing cross-spectrum confirmation of Israel's official position.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel said it rejected allegations of sexual abuse
Israel and Russia both deny the use of sexual violence by their military

claims.text: Israel's UN ambassador Danny Danon announced that Israel was cutting ties with UN Secretary General António Guterres in response to the blacklisting.
claims.rationale: Center (BBC) and center-left (Guardian, Haaretz) sources all confirm Danon's announcement of severed ties with Guterres.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel's ambassador accused Guterres of spreading antisemitic lies and said the Israeli UN mission will refuse contact with his office as long as he serves
Danny Danon said the country had cut ties with the UN secretary general in response to the blacklisting
Israeli ambassador made a statement about Israel being added to the UN blacklist

claims.text: Israel's stated justification for cutting ties with Guterres was that he was spreading antisemitic lies.
claims.rationale: Only BBC explicitly reports Danon's characterization of Guterres as spreading antisemitic lies as the stated reason for the break.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel's ambassador Danny Danon accused Guterres of spreading antisemitic lies

claims.text: Danny Danon stated that Israel submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim in the UN investigation.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports Danon's statement about Israel's alleged cooperation with the investigation.

framing_variants.characterization: Israel submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim

claims.text: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar announced a defamation lawsuit against the New York Times over a report based on testimonies of 14 Palestinian sexual abuse survivors.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the Netanyahu-Sa'ar defamation lawsuit announcement against the New York Times.

framing_variants.characterization: Netanyahu and Sa'ar said they were launching a defamation lawsuit, calling the report 'one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published against the state of Israel in the modern press'

claims.text: Five Israeli guards were filmed on leaked CCTV footage apparently sexually abusing a Palestinian detainee from Gaza at the Sde Teiman detention facility; a doctor found corroborating wounds, but Israel's top military lawyer dropped charges against the guards in March of this year.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the Sde Teiman incident, the medical evidence, and the dropping of charges, providing cross-spectrum confirmation.

framing_variants.characterization: Five guards caught on leaked CCTV appearing to sexually abuse detainee; doctor found corroborating wounds; charges dropped in March
Assault and rape filmed on security cameras, reported to police by Israeli medics; victim never charged or tried; has since been released

claims.text: Benjamin Netanyahu described the guards accused in the Sde Teiman incident as heroic and described the attempt to prosecute them as criminal.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports Netanyahu's characterizations of the Sde Teiman guards and the prosecution attempt.

framing_variants.characterization: Netanyahu described the alleged perpetrators as 'heroic' and the attempt to prosecute them as 'criminal'

claims.text: The UN identified a systemic lack of accountability for sexual violence in Israeli detention, contributing to a culture of impunity.
claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the UN's finding of a systemic accountability failure in Israeli detention.

framing_variants.characterization: Human rights organisations criticised a lack of accountability and apparent impunity for abuses committed by Israeli troops throughout the Gaza war
A 'systemic lack of accountability' for sexual violence helped create a culture of impunity

claims.text: Action on Armed Violence found that nearly 9 in 10 Israeli military investigations into crimes committed by soldiers since the start of the Gaza war were closed without finding fault or resolution, with only one prison conviction out of 52 cases studied.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the specific findings from Action on Armed Violence on Israeli military investigation outcomes.

framing_variants.characterization: Nearly nine in 10 Israeli investigations closed without finding fault; only one prison conviction out of 52 cases

claims.text: The Israeli military stated that it conducts examination and investigation processes regarding exceptional incidents with suspicion of law violations, opening criminal investigations when there is reasonable suspicion of an offence in accordance with Israeli and international law.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the Israeli military's official statement on its investigative procedures.

framing_variants.characterization: Military says it investigates exceptional incidents and opens criminal investigations under Israeli and international law

claims.text: The UN Committee against Torture stated in November that it was deeply concerned about reports indicating a de facto state policy of organised and widespread torture and ill treatment of Palestinian detainees in Israeli jails, which had gravely intensified after the Hamas-led attacks of 7 October 2023.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reports the UN Committee against Torture's November statement and its specific language.

framing_variants.characterization: UN Committee against Torture deeply concerned about de facto state policy of organised and widespread torture, which gravely intensified after 7 October 2023

claims.text: Rights groups characterize Israeli detention centres as having become torture camps for Palestinians, with violence including rape, extreme hunger and humiliation normalized over the past three years.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports rights groups' characterization of Israeli detention conditions using this specific framing.

framing_variants.characterization: Rights groups say detention centres have become 'torture camps'; violence including rape, extreme hunger and humiliation has been normalised

claims.text: Far-right Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir published footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists detained while trying to sail to Gaza with aid, causing a diplomatic crisis; at least 15 activists said they were sexually assaulted in custody, including at least one rape.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reports Ben-Gvir's publication of the footage, the diplomatic fallout, and the activists' allegations of sexual assault.

framing_variants.characterization: Ben-Gvir published footage causing a diplomatic crisis; at least 15 activists said they were sexually assaulted, including a rape

claims.text: Hamas was previously added to the UN sexual violence blacklist for the October 7, 2023 attacks on Israel and for abuse of hostages in Gaza.
claims.rationale: Center (BBC), center-left (Guardian), and center-right (i24 News) sources all confirm Hamas's prior inclusion on the blacklist, providing cross-spectrum agreement.

framing_variants.characterization: Hamas was added to the previous report last year; 12 former hostages made allegations of sexual violence against Hamas captors
The UN had already added Hamas to the sexual violence blacklist for the October 7 attacks and for abuse of hostages in Gaza
Hamas was included alongside Israel in a UN blacklisting for sex crimes, with i24 emphasizing the controversial grouping

framing_variants.characterization: Hamas has not recognised any cases of sexual violence or held any alleged perpetrators responsible

claims.rationale: Both BBC (center) and The Guardian (center-left) report the 310 verified cases with overlapping demographic and violation-type details.

framing_variants.characterization: 310 instances including rape and genital mutilation, affecting 280 men, 26 women and four girls
310 cases including rape, gang-rape, genital mutilation, and electric shocks to genitals; most victims were men

framing_variants.characterization: Russia deployed systematic sexual torture in 'almost all' detention centres; two-thirds of cases used multiple forms; more than half of survivors endured repeated attacks

claims.rationale: Only The Guardian specifically reports Russia's obstruction of UN monitors' access.

framing_variants.characterization: Russia obstructed investigations into 'systemic' sexual violence, barring monitors from accessing prisoners of war and civilians in detention

claims.rationale: Only BBC notes Russia's absence of public comment on the report.

framing_variants.characterization: Russia has not publicly commented

paragraphs.text: The United Nations added Israel and Russia to its annual sexual violence in conflict blacklist for the first time, according to its 2026 Conflict-Related Sexual Violence report. The report verified 31 cases of sexual violence by Israeli forces against Palestinians and 310 cases by Russian forces against Ukrainians. Both countries were added to a list that includes 77 parties in total, joining Hamas, which had been blacklisted previously for sexual violence during and after the October 7, 2023 attacks on Israel.

The UN verified 31 cases of conflict-related sexual violence perpetrated by Israeli forces against Palestinians from Gaza and the West Bank. Of these cases, 13 occurred in 2025 and 18 occurred in the two prior years. The victims included 14 men, 7 women, 9 boys, and 1 girl. The documented violations included rape, gang rape, rape with objects, forced nudity, attacks on and targeted shooting of genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, threats of rape, and attempted rape.

The perpetrators identified by the UN included Israeli military officers, police, prison service officers, and members of an elite police counter-terrorism unit. The UN documented nine cases of rape, mostly involving men or boys from Gaza, targeted in detention centres or during interrogation. One rape attack took place in a police station in the Gush Etzion settlement in the occupied West Bank. The UN characterized the documented cases as indicative of incidents and patterns rather than a comprehensive summary of all conflict-related sexual violence by Israeli forces.

The UN reported that the Israeli government obstructed its attempts to fully investigate sexual violence, including barring UN experts from detention centres and blocking travel to Gaza. Israel has not allowed the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit detainees since October 2023. The UN also said threats were made against Palestinian detainees to prevent them from reporting abuse after their release. The UN was unable to verify some allegations of sexual violence by Hamas in part because the Israeli government denied access to UN bodies to carry out investigations.

One documented incident involved five Israeli guards filmed on leaked CCTV footage apparently sexually abusing a Palestinian detainee from Gaza at the Sde Teiman detention facility. A doctor found corroborating wounds, but Israel's top military lawyer dropped charges against the guards in March of this year. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu described the guards as heroic and characterized the attempt to prosecute them as criminal.

The UN identified a systemic lack of accountability for sexual violence in Israeli detention, contributing to a culture of impunity. Independent research by Action on Armed Violence found that nearly 9 in 10 Israeli military investigations into crimes committed by soldiers since the start of the Gaza war were closed without finding fault or resolution, with only one prison conviction out of 52 cases studied. In November, the UN Committee against Torture stated it was deeply concerned about reports indicating a de facto state policy of organised and widespread torture and ill treatment of Palestinian detainees in Israeli jails, which had gravely intensified after the Hamas-led attacks of October 7, 2023.

A separate incident highlighting accountability concerns involved Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, who published footage of Israeli security forces abusing international activists detained while trying to sail to Gaza with aid, causing a diplomatic crisis. At least 15 activists said they were sexually assaulted in custody, including at least one rape.

The UN verified 310 cases of conflict-related sexual violence by Russian forces against Ukrainians, including rape, gang rape, genital mutilation, and applying electric shocks to genitals. The victims included 280 men, 26 women, and 4 girls. The UN described Russian sexual violence as systematic torture deployed in almost all detention centres, with two-thirds of cases involving multiple forms of sexual violence and more than half of survivors enduring repeated attacks. Russia obstructed UN investigations by barring monitors from accessing prisoners of war and civilians in detention. Russia has not publicly commented on the report.

Israel rejected the allegations of sexual abuse included in the UN report. Israeli UN ambassador Danny Danon stated that Israel submitted evidence, documents, and detailed responses to every claim in the UN investigation. However, an Israeli center-right outlet reported that a UN official stated they did not review evidence before blacklisting Israel — a procedural claim not confirmed or addressed by other sources across the spectrum.

Israel's response to the blacklisting was severe. Danny Danon announced that Israel was cutting ties with UN Secretary General António Guterres. One center outlet reported that Israel's stated justification was that Guterres was spreading antisemitic lies. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar also announced a defamation lawsuit against the New York Times over a report based on testimonies of 14 Palestinian sexual abuse survivors.

The Israeli military issued a statement asserting that it conducts examination and investigation processes regarding exceptional incidents with suspicion of law violations, opening criminal investigations when there is reasonable suspicion of an offence in accordance with Israeli and international law.

Hamas was previously added to the UN sexual violence blacklist for the October 7, 2023 attacks on Israel and for abuse of hostages in Gaza. Hamas has not recognized any cases of sexual violence and has not held any alleged perpetrators responsible.
Details of the UN report were shared by Israeli diplomats at the UN before its official release, and the full report was subsequently posted online by the US news site PassBlue.

There is a significant framing difference in how outlets across the spectrum characterize conditions in Israeli detention facilities. One center-left outlet reported that rights groups characterize Israeli detention centres as having become torture camps for Palestinians, with violence including rape, extreme hunger and humiliation normalized over the past three years. This characterization represents advocacy organizations' assessment of systemic patterns rather than the UN's case-by-case verification approach presented elsewhere in the report.
